title: Friendship has become "low-cost"
body:
We've reached a critical point. In theory, technology was supposed to "free" us from heavy work so we could dedicate ourselves to art and human relationships. But the reality is different: **we are delegating our humanity to robots because humans have become "unsustainable".**

Why are more and more people preferring to talk to an AI than call a friend?

-   **The refuge against *ghosting*:** AI doesn't disappear, doesn't leave you on "read" or isolate you when things get ugly. We've created algorithmic friends because flesh and blood ones fail us too often.
-   **The fear of being a burden:** We're afraid to bother people. We live so exhausted that asking for ten minutes of attention feels like a luxury we can't afford. Friendship has become a low-cost transaction where, at the slightest conflict or need, people disconnect.
-   **Empathy by subscription:** We've gone from the "tribe" to the psychologist, and from the psychologist to the chatbot. We seek a listener who doesn't judge us, but also one that doesn't demand anything from us in return.

**The paradox is tragic:** We have the best technology in history to connect, but we feel more alone than ever. We've chosen the comfort of code that pleases us over the difficulty of a real relationship that makes us grow.

AI can simulate company, but it can't hold your hand or look you in the eyes when the world collapses.

**Are we losing the muscle of commitment? Or have we decided that loving others costs too much?**
